author: @maranmarantakeiteazie
text: Rob, that is beautiful story and many thanks for sharing the moment. It's a shame that this type of thinking is not taught in school. Osho wrote about the Zen master Hyakujo and his practice of stopping to disengage from his automated processes to truly feel the moment and be present. I think Osho also told a story of enlightenment which sounds similar to your experience: Someone asked a Zen monk if could explain enlightenment. The monk said that it would be impossible to do that, but he could tell them when it happened - one day when chopping wood he was getting ready for the next swing when suddenly it occurred to him that he wasn't chopping the wood.
